stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The Atacama Desert in northern Chile is one of the driest places on Earth.
  • Some weather stations there have recorded average annual rainfall of less than 0.04 inches.
  • Specialized microbes that can survive long stretches without water inhabit the desert.
  • During rare wet periods, average rainfall can reach a relatively generous 0.6 inches.
  • About 200 different kinds of flowering plants are known to grow in the Atacama.
stem: The student wants to emphasize how dry the conditions can be in the Atacama Desert. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Microbes that can survive long stretches without water inhabit the Atacama.
Conditions in the Atacama can be extraordinarily dry, with some weather stations recording average annual rainfall of less than 0.04 inches.
There are about 200 different kinds of flowering plants that grow in the Atacama, where rainfall can reach a generous 0.6 inches in wet periods.
Along with about 200 different kinds of flowering plants, specialized microbes can live in the Atacama.

rationale: Choice B is the best answer because it specifically emphasizes the extreme dryness, citing the 0.04-inch annual rainfall figure. Choice A is incorrect because it discusses microbe adaptation rather than the dryness itself. Choice C is incorrect because it focuses on plant diversity and a relatively generous wet-period figure. Choice D is incorrect because it lists biological inhabitants without emphasizing dryness.
